pages.full_text:
__Sat 31st July.__

We set off at 4 pm, S, E, W, P, J, C,
(Thomas is at Jesta in Belgium + will join us later)
We motored up to __Orton__ near the __Lake District__
(J. [?M] 38 on M6) A beautiful campsite, [?view] of distant
hills, in fields with drystone walls. Good wine
at supper.
__Sun__
Warm at
breakfast time.
We drove off at
9.45 + had
lunch at the
Art Lover's Ho,
Glasgow.
Designed 1901
by Mackintosh +
MacDonald.
Built 1990's.

A gorgeous white
music room.
Wall hangings,
one embroidered,
others printed.
(Wm didn't like)
After a cup of

[Sketch caption, written vertically along right margin:] Piano + piano stool, white, mushroom, Art-Lover's House, Glasgow
pages.route_sketch_description: Hand-drawn sketch of an interior room — appears to be the music room of the Art-Lover's House, Glasgow. Shows furniture arrangement including piano and piano stool. Annotations on the sketch include 'lilac seat cushion', 'purple', 'green'. Caption written vertically: 'Piano + piano stool, white, mushroom, Art-Lover's House, Glasgow'.
pages.notes: No handwritten page number visible — top-left corner is cut off. The sketch is a room interior, not a route map, but it is hand-drawn with labels so route_sketch is set to true to flag it. 'Jesta' could be a place name in Belgium (possibly 'Gesta' or another reading). 'no showers' appears as a later annotation above 'Orton' and again above 'at supper'. The date 'Sat 31st July' with Monday 2nd August on the right page is consistent with multiple years; the Art Lover's House Glasgow was completed/opened 1996, so the year is 1999 or later — the 'Wales May 1999' photo on the right page and the narrative flow suggest this trip is likely summer 1999 or 2000.

pages.full_text:
tea we drove up to Balloch *** on lochlomond.
Very organised campsite, keys to showers. Ping pong.
The caretaker was extremely rude just before we
went to bed + there was a thunderstorm.

__Monday Aug 2nd__

We went back into Glasgow + parked by
the river + walked across an old pedestrian bridge,
up the hill to the School of Art tour at 11. Tonic
stayed in the loo in the van. We all enjoyed it. Than
[?We/Wh] [illegible] [illegible] [illegible] [illegible] [illegible] [illegible]
pages.notes: Photo at top of page shows a young man in front of a camper van; caption 'Wales May 1999' is written in green ink above the photo and appears to label this photograph — likely from a different trip inserted here. Balloch is on Loch Lomond. The caption along the left gutter 'Piano + piano stool, white, mushroom, Art-Lover's House, Glasgow' continues from the left page sketch. The bottom of the page is partially cut off; final line(s) are illegible. 'Monday Aug 2nd' following 'Sat 31st July' and 'Sunday' is internally consistent. Year cannot be confirmed from the text alone; the 'Wales May 1999' photo label is on a different trip's photo, but combined with the Art Lover's House opening ~1996, this entry is likely from summer 1999 or later.
